title: Phase 4 Review: Times, Openings & City Life

meta.focus: Review of Kanji, Vocabulary, and Grammar from Lessons N4.31 (Times & Openings: 代・森・堂・開) and N4.32 (Use, Learn, & The City: 引・用・習・市).

sections.title: Part 1: Conversation Check
sections.instructions: Read each conversation and choose the best answer to the question.

items.title: Conversation 1
items.context: A manager and a new staff member discuss a community gathering being held at the assembly hall in the forest.

lines.jp: 来週、森の 会堂で 会合が 開かれます。
開始は 何時ですか。代金も ありますか。
午前 十時に 開始します。食堂に 人を 集めさせました。
人が 集まれる いい 時代 だと 思います ね。

items.question: What is A organizing at the assembly hall in the forest?
choices: A community gathering (会合) starting at 10 a.m.
A music concert with a ticket booth.
A calligraphy class for the new staff.
A morning market in the city.
items.answer: A community gathering (会合) starting at 10 a.m.
items.explanation: A says 森の会堂で会合が開かれます (a meeting will be held at the assembly hall in the forest) and 午前十時に開始します (it starts at 10 a.m.).

items.title: Conversation 2 (Casual)
items.context: Two friends discuss plans to visit the morning market this weekend.

lines.jp: 週まつ、都市の 朝市に 行くんだけど、いっしょに こない？
いいね。でも、急用が あって、朝は 行けないんだ。
午後から 来られるように なったら、電話して。
分かった。あの 食堂に 行ったことが あるよ。代金が 安くて おいしかった。

items.question: Why can B not come in the morning?
choices: B has urgent business in the morning.
B is studying calligraphy all morning.
B has already visited the morning market.
B does not like the city dining hall.
items.answer: B has urgent business in the morning.
items.explanation: B says 急用があって、朝は行けないんだ — B has urgent business (急用) and cannot go in the morning.

items.title: Conversation 3 (Casual)
items.context: Two siblings talk at home about learning a new skill.

lines.jp: 日本語の 習字を 習って いるんだ。先生を 見習って 毎日 書いて いる。
へえ。むずかしいけど、毎日 勉強すれば 上手に なれると 思う。
うん。引き出しの 中に 習字の 本が あるから、それも 使おう と 思って いる。
前の 世代の 人から 習ったことが あるって 言って いたよね。

items.question: What is A planning to use to help with learning calligraphy?
choices: A calligraphy book found in a drawer.
A manual from the city office.
A guide from the morning market.
items.answer: A calligraphy book found in a drawer.
items.explanation: A says 引き出しの中に習字の本があるから、それも使おうと思っている — there is a calligraphy book (習字の本) in the drawer (引き出し), and A plans to use it.

sections.title: Part 2: Grammar Scramble
sections.instructions: Put the words in the correct order to build the sentence.

items.q: A music event was held in the main hall.
items.explanation: 'Ongaku no kai ga hondou de hirakaremashita' — 開く (to hold/open) in polite passive past (開かれました) is used because the hall is where the event was held. 本堂 = main hall (堂 N4.31).

items.q: The teacher made the students learn calligraphy.
items.explanation: 'Sensei wa gakusei ni shuuji wo narawasemashita' — causative past form 習わせました (made to learn) of 習う (to learn). This is the polite causative past form introduced in N4.31.

items.q: People can now use the city market.
items.explanation: 'Ichi no asaichi wo tsukaeru you ni narimashita' — ようになる (came to be able to) expresses the change in state. 使える is the potential form of 使う.

items.q: I have been into the forest before.
items.explanation: 'Mae ni mori no naka ni haitta koto ga arimasu' — たことがある (have done / have experienced) uses the plain past form 入った of 入る. 森 = forest (N4.31).

items.q: I think the way of using things has changed with the era.
segments: 代わったと 思います
items.explanation: 'Jidai to issho ni tsukaikata ga kawatta to omoimasu' — と思います (I think) after a plain past clause. 代わる = to change/substitute (代 N4.31), 使い方 = way of using (使 N4.1).

sections.title: Part 3: Reading — A Forest Dining Hall
sections.instructions: Read the passage and answer the questions below.

passage.jp: 森の 中に 古い 食堂が あります。
passage.en: There is an old dining hall inside the forest.

passage.jp: この 食堂の 代金は 安くて、毎日 多くの 人が 来ます。
passage.en: The fee at this dining hall is cheap, and many people come every day.

passage.jp: 食堂の 入り口では 用紙が 使われます。来た 人は 名前を 書かせられます。
passage.en: A form is used at the dining hall entrance. People who come are made to write their name.

passage.jp: この 食堂で 食事をしたことが ある 人は、また 来たいと 思うと 言います。
passage.en: People who have eaten at this dining hall say they think they want to come again.

passage.jp: 森林の 中の 食堂が 都市の 人々に 知られるように なりました。
passage.en: The dining hall in the forest has come to be known by people in the city.

questions.q: この 食堂は どこに ありますか。
questions.explanation: The first sentence of the passage says 森の中に古い食堂があります — the dining hall is inside the forest (森の中).

questions.q: 入り口で 何を させられますか。
questions.a: 名前を 書かせられます
choices: 名前を 書かせられます
questions.explanation: The third sentence says 名前を書かせられます — people who come are made to write (書かせられます, full causative-passive) their name (名前) on the form.

questions.q: この 食堂は どんな 人に 知られましたか。
questions.explanation: The final sentence says 都市の人々に知られるようになりました — the dining hall has come to be known by people in the city (都市の人々).

sections.title: Part 4: Kanji Readings
sections.instructions: Select the correct reading for the [bracketed] word.

items.q: 今の[時代]は 前と 代わりましたね。
items.explanation: 時代 (era / period) is read as jidai. 時 (N5.2) = time; 代 (N4.31) = era/substitute.

items.q: 山の 中に [森林]が あります。
items.explanation: 森林 (forest / woodland) is read as shinrin. 森 (N4.31) = forest; 林 (N4.25) = grove.

items.q: この [本堂]は 大きいです。
items.explanation: 本堂 (main hall of a temple) is read as hondou. 本 (N5.7) = main/book; 堂 (N4.31) = hall.

items.q: 店が [開店]しました。
items.explanation: 開店 (opening a shop / shop opening) is read as kaiten. 開 (N4.31) = open; 店 (N5.4) = shop.

items.q: ゆっくり [引き]ます。
items.explanation: 引きます (pull / draw a line) is read as hikimasu. 引 (N4.32) = pull/draw. The verb 引く is a godan verb.

items.q: 先生に [習字]を 習います。
items.explanation: 習字 (calligraphy) is read as shuuji. 習 (N4.32) = learn/practice; 字 (N4.22) = character/letter.

items.q: 今日は [用事] が あって、早く 帰ります。
items.explanation: 用事 (errand / business) is read as youji. 用 (N4.32) = use/service; 事 (N4.13) = thing/matter.

items.q: この [都市] は とても 大きいです。
items.explanation: 都市 (city / urban area) is read as toshi. 都 (N4.11) = capital/metropolis; 市 (N4.32) = city.

sections.title: Part 5: Context & Vocabulary
sections.instructions: Choose the best word to complete each sentence.

items.q: その 集会は 先週の 水曜日に ______。
items.explanation: 'Sono shuukai wa senshuu no suiyoubi ni hirakaremashita' — 開かれました is the polite passive past of 開く (to hold/open). The gathering was held (passive) last Wednesday. 集会 = gathering (N4.30).

items.q: 今日は ______ が あるので、早く 帰ります。
items.explanation: 'Kyou wa youji ga aru node, hayaku kaerimasu' — 用事 (errands / tasks / business) fits. The speaker has business to do and must leave early.

items.q: ______ の 中に 大切な 紙が 入って います。
items.explanation: 'Hikidashi no naka ni taisetsu na kami ga haitte imasu' — 引き出し (drawer) fits: important papers are stored inside the drawer. 引き出す = to pull out → 引き出し = drawer (N4.32).

items.q: 先生を ______ 毎日 書いて います。
items.explanation: 'Sensei wo minaratte mainichi kaite imasu' — 見習う (to emulate / to follow an example) fits: the speaker is emulating the teacher and writing every day. 見習う = 見 (observe) + 習う (learn) (N4.32).

sections.title: Part 6: Grammar Check
sections.instructions: Choose the correct grammar form to complete each sentence.

items.q: この 森の 中で、大きい 音楽の 集会が ______。
items.explanation: 'Kono mori no naka de, ookii ongaku no shuukai ga hirakaremashita' — the event was held (passively, from the venue's perspective), so the passive form 開かれました is correct. 開ける (transitive active) and 開く (intransitive or active) are not correct here.

items.q: 先生は 学生に 漢字を ______。
items.explanation: 'Sensei wa gakusei ni kanji wo kakasemashita' — causative past 書かせました (made to write) is correct because the teacher is causing the student to do the action. 書かれました is passive (was written). 書かせられました is causative-passive (was made to write by someone — wrong subject). 書きました is simple past active.

items.q: 都市の 朝市を 使えるように ______。
items.explanation: 'Toshi no asaichi wo tsukaeru you ni narimashita' — ようになる (came to be able to / came to do) uses なる to express a natural change of state. ようにする (using する) expresses deliberate effort rather than change.

items.q: この 時代は 大きく 代わった ______。
items.explanation: 'Kono jidai wa ookiku kawatta to omoimasu' — と思います (I think) follows a plain-past clause to express the speaker's opinion. The sentence means 'I think this era has changed greatly.' 代わる = to change/shift (代 N4.31) — its core sense is "to substitute or take over for something"; you will later meet 変わる for general state changes. と思う = I think (G27).
